content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings AAA and BBB, both of length NNN and containing only the characters `a`, `b`, and `c`.

In one move, you can modify string AAA as follows:


Choose three indices i,j,ki, j, ki,j,k (1≤i<j<k≤N1 \leq i  \lt j \lt k \leq N1≤i<j<k≤N) such that Ai=a,Aj=b,Ak=cA_i = \texttt{a}, A_j = \texttt{b}, A_k = \texttt{c}Ai​=a,Aj​=b,Ak​=c.

That is, choose some subsequence of AAA that is `abc`.
Then, swap the values of AiA_iAi​ and AkA_kAk​.

That is, turn the subsequence `abc` into `cba`.
For example, if A=ca‾cb‾ac‾bA = \texttt{c\underline{a}c\underline{b}a\underline{c}b}A=ca​cb​ac​b, in one move it can be converted into cc‾cb‾aa‾b\texttt{c\underline{c}c\underline{b}a\underline{a}b}cc​cb​aa​b by operating on the underlined subsequence `abc`.

You can perform this move as many times as you like (possibly, zero).

Is it possible to make AAA equal to BBB?

Note that you can only modify string AAA, and not BBB.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the lengths of strings AAA and BBB.
The second line contains the string AAA, of length NNN and containing only the characters `a`, `b`, and `c`.
The third line contains the string BBB, of length NNN and containing only the characters `a`, `b`, and `c`.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: `Yes` if AAA and BBB can be made equal, and `No` otherwise.


Each letter of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `no`, `NO`, `No`, and `nO` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​ will be one of `a`, `b`, `c`.
The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.input:
4
3
acb
abc
5
acbcb
ccbab
5
abacc
cbcaa
9
abcbaabcb
abcbaabcb
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: No operations can be performed, since there's no abc subsequence. AAA can't be made equal to BBB.
Test case 222: a‾cbc‾b→c‾cba‾b\texttt{\underline{a}c\underline{bc}b} \to \texttt{\underline{c}c\underline{ba}b}a​cbc​b→c​cba​b makes AAA equal to BBB.
Test case 333: It can be shown that there's no way to make AAA and BBB equal.
Test case 444: The strings are already equal.
